qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in remote URL lookup functionality
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The SSRF finding describes URL validation flaws in remote lookup functionality, which is completely different from the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a debug mode string parsing issue where Flask debug mode cannot be disabled due to config values being read as strings (CWE-489: Active Debug Code). The SSRF finding involves HTTP requests to arbitrary URLs without validation (CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery). These are distinct root causes affecting different code paths and requiring different fixes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a debug mode string parsing issue in unfurl/app.py where the 'debug' configuration value is read as a string and passed directly to app.run(debug=...), causing any non-empty string (including 'False') to evaluate as truthy and leave the Werkzeug debugger active. The only real qualifying finding (ssrf-in-remote-lookups) describes a Server-Side Request Forgery vulnerability in the remote URL lookup functionality, which is a completely different root cause, vulnerability class (CWE-918 vs CWE-489), affected code location, and security impact. The example folder is a template with placeholder data, not a real vulnerability finding.
evidence: Advisory describes debug mode configuration parsing bug in unfurl/app.py:web_app() where string values are passed to app.run(debug=...)
Advisory CVE-489: Active Debug Code - Werkzeug debugger cannot be disabled
SSRF finding describes CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery in URL validation logic for remote lookups
Different vulnerable code paths: advisory focuses on app.py debug config, SSRF finding focuses on parse_shortlink.py, parse_hash.py, parse_magnet.py URL request handling
Different security consequences: advisory leaves debugger active, SSRF finding enables internal network resource access
Example folder is a template with placeholder vulnerability_report.json and references to 'https://github.com/example/vulnerable-repo.git'
